steps.title: Evaluate both sides at x = 1 to create a constraint
steps.content:
Since the original expression must equal $$\frac{a}{4}x^{2}+\frac{b}{4}x+\frac{c}{4}$$ for all values of $x$, we can choose any convenient value. At $x = 1$:

Original expression: $$-16(5(1)-3)^{2}+4(5(1)-2)^{2} = -16(2)^{2}+4(3)^{2} = -16(4)+4(9) = -64+36 = -28$$

Form at $x = 1$: $$\frac{a}{4}(1)^{2}+\frac{b}{4}(1)+\frac{c}{4} = \frac{a+b+c}{4}$$

Setting them equal: $$\frac{a+b+c}{4} = -28$$

steps.title: Solve for a + b + c
steps.content:
Multiply both sides by 4:
$$a + b + c = -28 \times 4 = -112$$

That matches choice A — A is correct.
